Billing

superbill software therapists

Last updated: January 15, 2025 • 5 min read

superbill software therapists

You just finished a session and the client asks, “Can you give me something for insurance?”
You nod, scramble for a blank form, realize your ICD-10 is missing, and spend ten minutes handwriting a sheet the insurer will probably reject. Two months later the client calls: the claim was denied and you’re out $600. If that sounds familiar, this post walks you through the exact tools that turn that chaos into a 30-second click.

Quick verdict: SimplePractice is the fastest way to create a single, clean superbill; TherapyNotes wins if you batch-print for a full caseload each week. Both keep every required field HIPAA-secure and insurer-ready.

SimplePractice

Pricing
$39–$59 /mo for solo plans (billed monthly; no setup fee).

Core superbill features

  • Toggle “Enable Superbill” once—every client chart auto-pulls NPI, CPT, ICD-10, DOS, fee, license, address
  • One-click PDF generation; file auto-saves to client record
  • Client-portal download: clients log in, click “Documents,” and print or forward to insurer
  • Unlimited superbill templates; no extra charge per document

Pros

  • Fastest learning curve—built for therapists, not billers
  • No retyping: pulls data already in the note
  • Mobile app works the same way; generate while still in session
  • Free 30-day trial; cancel online (no phone call)

Cons

  • Batch operations limited to 20 at a time; bigger practices feel cramped
  • Branding options only on higher-tier plan
  • ERA tracking is separate add-on

Best for
Solo clinicians who want to press one button and be done.

Try SIMPLEPRACTICE free →

TherapyNotes

Pricing
$49 /mo solo (includes notes, scheduling, telehealth, unlimited claims).

Core superbill features

  • Batch print or e-mail superbills for the entire week in one screen
  • Custom header/logo, footer disclaimer, and color palette
  • Integrated ERA tracking—see when payer acknowledges the superbill
  • Built-in CMS 1500 cross-check warns if required field differs between forms

Pros

  • Handles 100+ superbills without timing out
  • Lets you brand the PDF so clients recognize your practice
  • ERA shows if insurer rejected superbill before client mails it
  • Unlimited document storage

Cons

  • Interface looks dated; more menu clicks to reach superbill generator
  • No client portal download—must e-mail or hand PDF to client
  • Slightly steeper learning curve for non-billers

Best for
High-volume practices that print weekly stacks or want ERA follow-up.

Try THERAPYNOTES free →

Head-to-head comparison

FeatureSimplePracticeTherapyNotes
Monthly solo cost$39–$59$49
One-click PDF✅✅
Batch print limit20Unlimited
Client portal download✅❌
Custom brandingHigher tier onlyIncluded
ERA trackingAdd-onIncluded
Mobile superbill✅❌
Free trial length30 days30 days

Who should choose which

Pick SimplePractice if you…

  • See 0–20 clients/week
  • Want the fastest path from session to PDF
  • Need clients to download their own forms

Pick TherapyNotes if you…

  • Print weekly superbills for 20+ clients
  • Want ERA updates to confirm payer receipt
  • Care about branded documents

FAQ

Does a superbill need a diagnosis code?
Yes. Insurers require an ICD-10 code that matches the date of service; both programs pull it automatically from your note.

What is the difference between a superbill and a CMS 1500?
A superbill is an itemized receipt the client submits; a CMS 1500 is a claim form the provider usually submits electronically. Use a superbill when you are out-of-network and the client pays you up front.

Can I create a superbill in SimplePractice for free?
During the 30-day trial you can generate unlimited superbills without entering a credit card.

How long does insurance take to reimburse a superbill?
Most commercial plans process out-of-network claims in 14–30 days if the form is complete; missing NPI or CPT can double that time.

What happens if the client loses the superbill?
Log back in, re-print or e-mail another copy; both platforms store the PDF indefinitely.

Conclusion

Stop handwriting forms and hoping insurers accept them. Pick SimplePractice for the simplest workflow or TherapyNotes for bulk printing and ERA tracking—either way you stay under $60 a month and keep every required field insurer-ready.
